# Preview: Spain vs Cape Verde - prediction, team news, lineups | 2026 World Cup

By Matt Law| 13 Jun 2026 14:45, Last updated: 14 Jun 2026 11:40

© Imago

Spain will be bidding to make the perfect start to their 2026 World Cup campaign when they take on tournament debutants Cape Verde in Atlanta on Monday.

La Roja will also take on Saudi Arabia and Uruguay in Group H this summer, and Luis de la Fuente's team are one of the favourites to triumph at the competition.

---

## Match preview

Spain vs. Cape Verde World Cup Preview And Predictions

Alongside France, Spain are considered to be the favourites for the 2026 World Cup, with head coach De la Fuente boasting a spectacular squad, and La Roja are the reigning European champions, beating England in the final of Euro 2024.

Spain won the World Cup in 2010 but have disappointed in their last three tournaments, being knocked out in the group stage in 2014 and then only making the round of 16 in 2018 and 2022, so there is room for serious improvement this summer.

La Roja will enter this match of

Squawka/ News/ Spain vs Cape Verde team news and predicted lineups

# Spain vs Cape Verde team news and predicted lineups

- Jonathan Davies
- 10 minutes ago

- Copied

Spain launch their 2026 World Cup campaign on Monday when they go head to head with debutants Cape Verde at 5pm (UK time).

Champions in 2010 and the reigning kings of Europe, Luis de la Fuente’s side are among the favourites to go all the way to glory in North America. Although, La Roja’s recent World Cup record is incredibly modest by their standards. Their title defence ended at the group stage in 2014, while their last two appearances have yielded successive last-16 exits on penalties.

Cape Verde, meanwhile, are the third-smallest nation and 14th from Africa to appear at the finals, having topped a CAF qualifying group ahead of former quarter-finalists Cameroon. Led by former captain Bubista, the Blue Sharks will look to follow in the footsteps of fellow African side Senegal by defe

news_items.title: Spain back at full strength ahead of its World Cup opener against Cape Verde - Yahoo Sports
news_items.author: TALES AZZONI Fri, June 12, 2026 at 10:06 PM UTC · 2 min read
news_items.text:
Spain back at full strength ahead of its World Cup opener against Cape Verde - Yahoo Sports

Story by

GUADALAJARA, Mexico (AP) — Spain will have a full squad available for its World Cup opener against Cape Verde on Monday with forward Víctor Muñoz returning to training a day after Lamine Yamal and Nico Williams also were back.

Spain was hit by injuries late last season and had to adjust its preparations in the run-up to soccer’s biggest tournament. Mikel Merino and Fabián Ruiz also dealt with injury setbacks before returning to the squad. One player who did not make it in time was Barcelona midfielder Fermín López, who has a fractured foot.

Advertisement

Advertisement

The biggest concern for Spain had been with Yamal, the young Barcelona winger who injured his left hamstring in a Spanish league game in April.

The 18-year-old Yamal is expected to lead the team as it tries to win its second World Cup title. Spain won soccer's greatest prize in South Africa in 2010, w

news_items.title: Spain trio, including Lamine Yamal, on course for World Cup opener - De la Fuente
news_items.author: News Desk
news_items.text:
Spain trio, including Lamine Yamal, on course for World Cup opener - De la Fuente

Lamine Yamal is set to be fit for the World Cup opener.

PUEBLA, Mexico, June 7 (Reuters) – Spain coach Luis de la Fuente said on Sunday that Lamine Yamal, Nico Williams and Victor Munoz are on track to be available for their World Cup opener against Cape Verde on June 15.

Yamal, 18, has been managing groin issues and a hamstring injury suffered in April, while Williams and Munoz have also been recovering from injuries.

The trio remained at Spain’s training base in Chattanooga, Tennessee, to continue their rehabilitation rather than travel with the squad for Monday’s final warm-up match against Peru.

“If there are no setbacks in the coming days, we expect them to be available on the 15th,” De la Fuente told reporters.

“I think all three will be in a position to play.”

Asked whether the players could be ready to start against Cape Verde, De la Fuente was more cautious.

READ: The best 10 World Cup kits including THAT Curacao away shirt

“All three are within the expected timelines to arrive in good condition for June 15,” he said.

“The most important thing is that those timelines are being met a

# Spain vs Cape Verde, FIFA World Cup 2026: Preview, team news, predicted lineups and prediction - Sports Yaari

Published: 2026-06-14T16:04:52+05:30
Source: sportsyaari.com (sportsyaari.com)
Language: en

## Story

Spain vs Cape Verde, FIFA World Cup 2026: Preview, team news, predicted lineups and prediction - Sports Yaari

# Spain vs Cape Verde, FIFA World Cup 2026: Preview, team news, predicted lineups and prediction

Published: 14 Jun 2026, 04:04 PM

Updated: 14 Jun 2026, 04:23 PM

Spain look to extend their unbeaten run (Image source: X)

Monday brings a clash unlike any seen before in recent World Cups. Deep inside Georgia, Spain - long considered giants of European football - starts its journey through Group H. Across from them stands Cape Verde, stepping onto the global stage for the very first time.

Looks like an uneven contest at first glance. Spain brings years of success, a name known worldwide. Cape Verde steps in fresh, proud of reaching this level for the very first time. As teams gather in Atlanta, Uruguay and Saudi Arabia, rounding out Group H, each has its own path forward. One carries the weight of history, the other hunger for recognition. Expectations stretch

PUEBLA, Mexico, June 7 (Reuters) – Spain coach Luis de la Fuente said on Sunday that Lamine Yamal, Nico Williams and Victor Munoz are on track to be available for their World Cup opener against Cape Verde on June 15.

Yamal, 18, has been managing groin issues and a hamstring injury suffered in April, while Williams and Munoz have also been recovering from injuries.

The trio remained at Spain’s training base in Chattanooga, Tennessee, to continue their rehabilitation rather than travel with the squad for Monday’s final warm-up match against Peru.

“If there are no setbacks in the coming days, we expect them to be available on the 15th,” De la Fuente told reporters.

“I think all three will be in a position to play.”

Asked whether the players could be ready to start against Cape Verde, De la Fuente was more cautious.
